FsiEvaluationSession.PartialAssemblySignatureUpdated 
PartialAssemblySignatureUpdated 
 Raised when an interaction is successfully typechecked and executed, resulting in an update to the
 type checking state.

 This event is triggered after parsing and checking, either via input from 'stdin', or via a call to EvalInteraction.

### [FsiEvaluationSession.GetDefaultConfiguration](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsession.html#GetDefaultConfiguration)

FsiEvaluationSession.GetDefaultConfiguration 
GetDefaultConfiguration 
 Get a configuration that uses the 'fsi' object (normally from FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll,
 an object from another DLL with identical characteristics) to provide an implementation of the configuration.
 FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll  is referenced by default.

### [FsiEvaluationSession.GetDefaultConfiguration](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsession.html#GetDefaultConfiguration)

FsiEvaluationSession.GetDefaultConfiguration 
GetDefaultConfiguration 
 Get a configuration that uses the 'fsi' object (normally from FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll,
 an object from another DLL with identical characteristics) to provide an implementation of the configuration.
 The flag indicates if FSharp.Compiler.Interactive.Settings.dll  is referenced by default.

### [FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.EventLoopInvoke](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsessionhostconfig.html#EventLoopInvoke)

### [FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.EventLoopRun](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsessionhostconfig.html#EventLoopRun)

FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.EventLoopRun 
EventLoopRun 
 Called by the evaluation session to ask the host to enter a dispatch loop like Application.Run().
 Only called if --gui option is used (which is the default).
 Gets called towards the end of startup and every time a ThreadAbort escaped to the backup driver loop.
 Return true if a 'restart' is required, which is a bit meaningless.

### [FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.EventLoopScheduleRestart](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsessionhostconfig.html#EventLoopScheduleRestart)

FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.EventLoopScheduleRestart 
EventLoopScheduleRestart 
 Schedule a restart for the event loop.

### [FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.GetOptionalConsoleReadLine](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-interactive-shell-fsievaluationsessionhostconfig.html#GetOptionalConsoleReadLine)

FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.GetOptionalConsoleReadLine 
GetOptionalConsoleReadLine 
<p class='fsdocs-para'>Indicate a special console "readline" reader for the evaluation session, if any.</p><p class='fsdocs-para'> </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>A "console" gets used if --readline is specified (the default on Windows + .NET); and --fsi-server is  not
 given (always combine with --readline-), and OptionalConsoleReadLine is given.
 When a console is used, special rules apply to "peekahead", which allows early typing on the console.
 Peekahead happens if --peekahead- is not specified (the default).
 In this case, a prompt is printed early, a background thread is created and
 the OptionalConsoleReadLine is used to read the first line.
 If a console is not used, then inReader.Peek() is called early instead.
 </p><p class='fsdocs-para'> </p><p class='fsdocs-para'>Further lines are read using OptionalConsoleReadLine().
 If not provided, lines are read using inReader.ReadLine().</p><p class='fsdocs-para'> </p>

FsiEvaluationSessionHostConfig.ReportUserCommandLineArgs 
ReportUserCommandLineArgs 
 The evaluation session calls this to report the preferred view of the command line arguments after
 stripping things like "/use:file.fsx", "-r:Foo.dll" etc.

### [FSharpExprPatterns.(|DecisionTree|_|)](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpexprpatterns.html#(|DecisionTree|_|))

FSharpExprPatterns.(|DecisionTree|_|) 
(|DecisionTree|_|) 
 Matches expressions with a decision expression, each branch of which ends in DecisionTreeSuccess passing control and values to one of the targets.

### [FSharpExprPatterns.(|DecisionTreeSuccess|_|)](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpexprpatterns.html#(|DecisionTreeSuccess|_|))

FSharpExprPatterns.(|DecisionTreeSuccess|_|) 
(|DecisionTreeSuccess|_|) 
 Special expressions at the end of a conditional decision structure in the decision expression node of a DecisionTree .
 The given expressions are passed as values to the decision tree target.

FSharpExprPatterns.(|Call|_|) 
(|Call|_|) 
 Matches expressions which are calls to members or module-defined functions. When calling curried functions and members the
 arguments are collapsed to a single collection of arguments, as done in the compiled version of these.

### [FSharpDisplayContext](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html)

FSharpDisplayContext 
 Represents the information needed to format types and other information in a style
 suitable for use in F# source text at a particular source location.

 Acquired via GetDisplayEnvAtLocationAlternate and similar methods. May be passed
 to the Format method on FSharpType and other methods. 
FSharpDisplayContext.WithPrefixGenericParameters 
WithPrefixGenericParameters 
FSharpDisplayContext.WithShortTypeNames 
WithShortTypeNames 
FSharpDisplayContext.WithSuffixGenericParameters 
WithSuffixGenericParameters 
FSharpDisplayContext.WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters 
WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters 
FSharpDisplayContext.Empty 
Empty

### [FSharpDisplayContext.WithPrefixGenericParameters](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html#WithPrefixGenericParameters)

FSharpDisplayContext.WithPrefixGenericParameters 
WithPrefixGenericParameters 
 Causes type signatures to be formatted with prefix-style generic parameters,
 for example `list<int>`.

### [FSharpDisplayContext.WithShortTypeNames](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html#WithShortTypeNames)

FSharpDisplayContext.WithShortTypeNames 
WithShortTypeNames

### [FSharpDisplayContext.WithSuffixGenericParameters](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html#WithSuffixGenericParameters)

FSharpDisplayContext.WithSuffixGenericParameters 
WithSuffixGenericParameters 
 Causes type signatures to be formatted with suffix-style generic parameters,
 for example `int list`

### [FSharpDisplayContext.WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html#WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters)

FSharpDisplayContext.WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters 
WithTopLevelPrefixGenericParameters 
 Causes type signatures to be formatted with prefix-style generic parameters for a top level type
 while nested types inherit generic parameters style from the current `FSharpDisplayContext` instance,
 for example, `int list seq` becomes `seq<int list>`

### [FSharpDisplayContext.Empty](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpdisplaycontext.html#Empty)

FSharpDisplayContext.Empty 
Empty

### [FSharpEntity.FSharpFields](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpentity.html#FSharpFields)

FSharpEntity.FSharpFields 
FSharpFields 
 Get the fields of a record, class, struct or enum from the perspective of the F# language.
 This includes static fields, the 'val' bindings in classes and structs, and the value definitions in enums.
 For classes, the list may include compiler generated fields implied by the use of primary constructors.

### [FSharpExpr](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpexpr.html)

FSharpExpr 
 Represents a checked and reduced expression, as seen by the F# language.  The active patterns
 in 'FSharp.Compiler.Symbols.FSharpExprPatterns' can be used to analyze information about the expression.

 Pattern matching is reduced to decision trees and conditional tests. Some other
 constructs may be represented in reduced form. 
FSharpExpr.ImmediateSubExpressions

Type

Range

### [FSharpMemberOrFunctionOrValue.CurriedParameterGroups](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpmemberorfunctionorvalue.html#CurriedParameterGroups)

FSharpMemberOrFunctionOrValue.CurriedParameterGroups 
CurriedParameterGroups 
List of list of parameters, where each nested item represents a defined parameter 

 Typically, there is only one nested list.
 However, code such as 'f (a, b) (c, d)' contains two groups, each with two parameters.
 In that example, there is a list made up of two lists, each with a parameter.

FSharpMemberOrFunctionOrValue.EventIsStandard 
EventIsStandard 
 Indicate if an event can be considered to be a property for the F# type system of type IEvent or IDelegateEvent.
 In this case ReturnParameter will have a type corresponding to the property type.  For
 non-standard events, ReturnParameter will have a type corresponding to the delegate type.

FSharpMemberOrFunctionOrValue.EventForFSharpProperty 
EventForFSharpProperty 
 Gets the event symbol implied by the use of a property,
 for the case where the property is actually an F#-declared CLIEvent.

 Uses of F#-declared events are considered to be properties as far as the language specification
 and this API are concerned.

### [FSharpSymbol](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html)

FSharpSymbol 
 Represents a symbol in checked F# source code or a compiled .NET component.

 The subtype of the symbol may reveal further information and can be one of FSharpEntity, FSharpUnionCase
 FSharpField, FSharpGenericParameter, FSharpStaticParameter, FSharpMemberOrFunctionOrValue, FSharpParameter,
 or FSharpActivePatternCase. 
FSharpSymbol.GetEffectivelySameAsHash 
GetEffectivelySameAsHash 
FSharpSymbol.HasAttribute 
HasAttribute 
FSharpSymbol.IsAccessible 
IsAccessible 
FSharpSymbol.IsEffectivelySameAs 
IsEffectivelySameAs 
FSharpSymbol.TryGetAttribute 
TryGetAttribute 
FSharpSymbol.DisplayNameCore 
DisplayNameCore 
FSharpSymbol.Accessibility 
Accessibility 
FSharpSymbol.Attributes 
Attributes 
FSharpSymbol.ImplementationLocation 
ImplementationLocation 
FSharpSymbol.Assembly 
Assembly 
FSharpSymbol.FullName 
FullName 
FSharpSymbol.DeclarationLocation 
DeclarationLocation 
FSharpSymbol.IsExplicitlySuppressed 
IsExplicitlySuppressed 
FSharpSymbol.DisplayName 
DisplayName 
FSharpSymbol.SignatureLocation 
SignatureLocation

### [FSharpSymbol.GetEffectivelySameAsHash](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#GetEffectivelySameAsHash)

FSharpSymbol.GetEffectivelySameAsHash 
GetEffectivelySameAsHash 
 A hash compatible with the IsEffectivelySameAs relation

### [FSharpSymbol.HasAttribute](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#HasAttribute)

FSharpSymbol.HasAttribute 
HasAttribute 
 Indicates if this symbol has an attribute matching the full name of the given type parameter

### [FSharpSymbol.IsAccessible](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#IsAccessible)

FSharpSymbol.IsAccessible 
IsAccessible 
 Computes if the symbol is accessible for the given accessibility rights

### [FSharpSymbol.IsEffectivelySameAs](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#IsEffectivelySameAs)

FSharpSymbol.IsEffectivelySameAs 
IsEffectivelySameAs 
 Return true if two symbols are effectively the same when referred to in F# source code text.
 This sees through signatures (a symbol in a signature will be considered effectively the same as
 the matching symbol in an implementation).  In addition, other equivalences are applied
 when the same F# source text implies the same declaration name - for example, constructors
 are considered to be effectively the same symbol as the corresponding type definition.

 This is the relation used by GetUsesOfSymbol and GetUsesOfSymbolInFile.

### [FSharpSymbol.TryGetAttribute](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#TryGetAttribute)

FSharpSymbol.TryGetAttribute 
TryGetAttribute 
 Try to get an attribute matching the full name of the given type parameter

### [FSharpSymbol.DisplayNameCore](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#DisplayNameCore)

FSharpSymbol.DisplayNameCore 
DisplayNameCore 
 Gets the display name for the symbol where double backticks are not added for non-identifiers

### [FSharpSymbol.Accessibility](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#Accessibility)

FSharpSymbol.Accessibility 
Accessibility 
 Get the declared accessibility of the symbol, if any

### [FSharpSymbol.Attributes](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#Attributes)

FSharpSymbol.Attributes 
Attributes 
 Get the attributes for the symbol, if any

### [FSharpSymbol.ImplementationLocation](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#ImplementationLocation)

FSharpSymbol.ImplementationLocation 
ImplementationLocation

### [FSharpSymbol.Assembly](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#Assembly)

FSharpSymbol.Assembly

 Get the assembly declaring this symbol

### [FSharpSymbol.FullName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#FullName)

FSharpSymbol.FullName 
FullName 
 Get a textual representation of the full name of the symbol. The text returned for some symbols
 may not be a valid identifier path in F# code, but rather a human-readable representation of the symbol.

### [FSharpSymbol.DisplayName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-symbols-fsharpsymbol.html#DisplayName)

FSharpSymbol.DisplayName 
DisplayName 
 Gets the display name for the symbol. Double backticks are added if the name is not a valid identifier.

 For FSharpParameter symbols without a name for the parameter, this returns "````"

 systematically with lower-case type inference variables such as <c>'a</c>.

### [ParsedInput](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html)

ParsedInput 

 Holds operations for working with the
 untyped abstract syntax tree (<a href="https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinput.html">ParsedInput</a>).
  
ParsedInput.exists 
exists 
ParsedInput.fold 
fold 
ParsedInput.foldWhile 
foldWhile 
ParsedInput.tryNode 
tryNode 
ParsedInput.tryPick 
tryPick 
ParsedInput.tryPickLast 
tryPickLast

### [ParsedInput.exists](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#exists)

ParsedInput.exists 
exists 

 Applies the given predicate to each node of the AST and its context (path)
 down to a given position, returning true if a matching node is found, otherwise false.
 Traversal is short-circuited if no matching node is found through the given position.

### [ParsedInput.fold](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#fold)

ParsedInput.fold 
fold 

 Applies a function to each node of the AST and its context (path),
 threading an accumulator through the computation.

### [ParsedInput.foldWhile](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#foldWhile)

ParsedInput.foldWhile 
foldWhile 

 Applies a function to each node of the AST and its context (path)
 until the folder returns <code>None</code>, threading an accumulator through the computation.

### [ParsedInput.tryNode](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#tryNode)

ParsedInput.tryNode 
tryNode 

 Dives to the deepest node that contains the given position,
 returning the node and its path if found, or <code>None</code> if no
 node contains the position.

### [ParsedInput.tryPick](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#tryPick)

ParsedInput.tryPick 
tryPick 

 Applies the given function to each node of the AST and its context (path)
 down to a given position, returning <code>Some x</code> for the first node
 for which the function returns <code>Some x</code> for some value <code>x</code>, otherwise <code>None</code>.
 Traversal is short-circuited if no matching node is found through the given position.

### [ParsedInput.tryPickLast](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-parsedinputmodule.html#tryPickLast)

ParsedInput.tryPickLast 
tryPickLast 

 Applies the given function to each node of the AST and its context (path)
 down to a given position, returning <code>Some x</code> for the last (deepest) node
 for which the function returns <code>Some x</code> for some value <code>x</code>, otherwise <code>None</code>.
 Traversal is short-circuited if no matching node is found through the given position.

### [PrettyNaming](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html)

 Some general F# utilities for mangling / unmangling / manipulating names.

### [PrettyNaming.IsOperatorDisplayName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#IsOperatorDisplayName)

PrettyNaming.IsOperatorDisplayName 
IsOperatorDisplayName 
<pre> Returns `true` if given string is an operator display name, e.g.
    ( |>> )
    |>>
    ..</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.IsIdentifierName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#IsIdentifierName)

PrettyNaming.IsIdentifierName 
IsIdentifierName 
<pre> Is the name a valid F# identifier, primarily used internally in PrettyNaming.fs for determining if an
 identifier needs backticks.

 In general do not use this routine. It is only used in one quick fix, for determining if it is valid
 to add "_" in front of an identifier.

     A            --> true
     A'           --> true
     _A           --> true
     A0           --> true
     |A|B|        --> false
     op_Addition  --> true
     +            --> false
     let          --> false
     base         --> false

 TBD: needs unit testing</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.IsActivePatternName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#IsActivePatternName)

PrettyNaming.IsActivePatternName 
IsActivePatternName 
<pre> Determines if the specified name is a valid name for an active pattern.
     |A|_|        --> true
     |A|B|        --> true
     |A|          --> true
     |            --> false
     ||           --> false
     op_Addition  --> false

 TBD: needs unit testing</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.DoesIdentifierNeedBackticks](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#DoesIdentifierNeedBackticks)

PrettyNaming.DoesIdentifierNeedBackticks 
DoesIdentifierNeedBackticks

### [PrettyNaming.NormalizeIdentifierBackticks](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#NormalizeIdentifierBackticks)

PrettyNaming.NormalizeIdentifierBackticks 
NormalizeIdentifierBackticks 
<pre> Adds double backticks if necessary to make a valid identifier, e.g.
     op_Addition  -->  op_Addition
     +            -->  ``+``    (this is not op_Addition)
     |>>          -->  ``|>>``  (this is not an op_)
     A-B          -->  ``A-B``
     AB           -->  AB
     |A|_|        -->  |A|_|    this is an active pattern name, needs parens not backticks
 Removes double backticks if not necessary to make a valid identifier, e.g.
     ``A``        --> A
     ``A-B``      --> ``A-B``</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.IsLogicalOpName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#IsLogicalOpName)

PrettyNaming.IsLogicalOpName 
IsLogicalOpName 
<pre> Is the name a logical operator name, including unary, binary and ternary operators
    op_UnaryPlus         - yes
    op_Addition          - yes
    op_Range             - yes (?)
    op_RangeStep         - yes (?)
    op_DynamicAssignment - yes
    op_Quack             - no
    +                    - no
    ABC                  - no
    ABC DEF              - no
    base                 - no
    |A|_|                - no</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.CompileOpName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#CompileOpName)

PrettyNaming.CompileOpName 
CompileOpName 
<pre> Converts the core of an operator name into a logical name. For example,
    +  --> op_Addition
    !%  --> op_DereferencePercent
 Only used on actual operator names</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.ConvertValLogicalNameToDisplayNameCore](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#ConvertValLogicalNameToDisplayNameCore)

PrettyNaming.ConvertValLogicalNameToDisplayNameCore 
ConvertValLogicalNameToDisplayNameCore 
<pre> Converts the logical name for and operator back into the core of a display name. For example:
     Foo                   --> Foo
     +                     --> +
     op_Addition           --> +
     op_DereferencePercent --> !%
     A-B                   --> A-B
     |A|_|                 --> |A|_|
     base                  --> base        regardless of IsBaseVal
 Used on names of all kinds

 TODO: We should assess uses of this function.

 In any cases it is used it probably indicates that text is being
 generated which:
    1. does not contain double-backticks for non-identifiers
    2. does not put parentheses around operators or active pattern names

 If the text is immediately in quotes, this is generally ok, e.g.

         error FS0038: '+' is bound twice in this pattern
         error FS0038: '|A|_|' is bound twice in this pattern
         error FS0038: 'a a' is bound twice in this pattern

 If not, the it is likely this should be replaced by ConvertValLogicalNameToDisplayName.</pre>

### [PrettyNaming.IsIdentifierFirstCharacter](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-prettynaming.html#IsIdentifierFirstCharacter)

PrettyNaming.IsIdentifierFirstCharacter 
IsIdentifierFirstCharacter

### [ExprAtomicFlag](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-expratomicflag.html)

ExprAtomicFlag 
 Indicates if an expression is an atomic expression.

 An atomic expression has no whitespace unless enclosed in parentheses, e.g.
 1, "3", ident, ident.[expr] and (expr). If an atomic expression has type T,
 then the largest expression ending at the same range as the atomic expression
 also has type T. 
ExprAtomicFlag.Atomic 
Atomic 
ExprAtomicFlag.NonAtomic 
NonAtomic

### [ExprAtomicFlag.Atomic](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-expratomicflag.html#Atomic)

ExprAtomicFlag.Atomic 
Atomic

### [ExprAtomicFlag.NonAtomic](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-expratomicflag.html#NonAtomic)

ExprAtomicFlag.NonAtomic 
NonAtomic

### [SynComponentInfo](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-syncomponentinfo.html)

SynComponentInfo 
 Represents the syntax tree associated with the name of a type definition or module
 in signature or implementation.

 This includes the name, attributes, type parameters, constraints, documentation and accessibility
 for a type definition or module. For modules, entries such as the type parameters are
 always empty. 
SynComponentInfo.Range 
Range 
SynComponentInfo.SynComponentInfo 
SynComponentInfo

### [SynConst.Bytes](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synconst.html#Bytes)

SynConst.Bytes 
Bytes 
 F# syntax: verbatim or regular byte string, e.g. "abc"B.

 Also used internally in the typechecker once an array of unit16 constants
 is detected, to allow more efficient processing of large arrays of uint16 constants.

 is detected, to allow more efficient processing of large arrays of uint16 constants.

SynExpr.Paren 
Paren 
 F# syntax: (expr)

 Parenthesized expressions. Kept in AST to distinguish A.M((x, y))
 from A.M(x, y), among other things.

SynExpr.Record 
Record 
 F# syntax: { f1=e1; ...; fn=en }
 inherit includes location of separator (for tooling)
 copyOpt contains range of the following WITH part (for tooling)
 every field includes range of separator after the field (for tooling)

SynExpr.IndexRange 
IndexRange 
 F# syntax: expr..
 F# syntax: ..expr
 F# syntax: expr..expr
 F# syntax: *
 A two-element range indexer argument a..b, a.., ..b. Also used to represent
 a range in a list, array or sequence expression.

### [SynExpr.Lambda](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synexpr.html#Lambda)

SynExpr.Lambda 
Lambda 
 First bool indicates if lambda originates from a method. Patterns here are always "simple"
 Second bool indicates if this is a "later" part of an iterated sequence of lambdas
 parsedData keeps original parsed patterns and expression,
 prior to transforming to "simple" patterns and iterated lambdas

 F# syntax: fun pat -> expr

### [SynExpr.App](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synexpr.html#App)

SynExpr.App 
App 
 F# syntax: f x

 flag: indicates if the application is syntactically atomic, e.g. f.[1] is atomic, but 'f x' is not
 isInfix is true for the first app of an infix operator, e.g. 1+2
 becomes App(App(+, 1), 2), where the inner node is marked isInfix

### [SynLongIdent](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synlongident.html)

SynLongIdent 
 Represents a long identifier with possible '.' at end.

 Typically dotRanges.Length = lid.Length-1, but they may be same if (incomplete) code ends in a dot, e.g. "Foo.Bar."
 The dots mostly matter for parsing, and are typically ignored by the typechecker, but
 if dotRanges.Length = lid.Length, then the parser must have reported an error, so the typechecker is allowed
 more freedom about typechecking these expressions.
 LongIdent can be empty list - it is used to denote that name of some AST element is absent (i.e. empty type name in inherit) 
SynLongIdent.ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd 
ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd 
SynLongIdent.Trivia 
Trivia 
SynLongIdent.Dots 
Dots 
SynLongIdent.LongIdent 
LongIdent 
SynLongIdent.Range 
Range 
SynLongIdent.IdentsWithTrivia 
IdentsWithTrivia 
SynLongIdent.RangeWithoutAnyExtraDot 
RangeWithoutAnyExtraDot 
SynLongIdent.SynLongIdent 
SynLongIdent

### [SynLongIdent.ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synlongident.html#ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd)

SynLongIdent.ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd 
ThereIsAnExtraDotAtTheEnd 
 Indicates if the construct ends in '.' due to error recovery

SynModuleDecl.Expr 
Expr 
 An 'expr' within a module.

### [SynModuleDecl.Types](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synmoduledecl.html#Types)

SynModuleDecl.Types 
Types 

 A type definition group ('<code>type T1 ... and T2 ...</code>') or a single '<code>type</code>' definition within a module.
  

 Consecutive '<code>type</code>' keywords (e.g. <code>type T1 ... type T2 ...</code>) are represented individually, with
 separate <code>Types</code> syntax tree nodes for each.
 Only the '<code>and</code>' keyword causes multiple types to be aggregated into a single <code>Types</code> node.

### [SynModuleDecl.Exception](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synmoduledecl.html#Exception)

SynModuleDecl.Exception 
Exception 
 An 'exception' definition within a module

### [SynModuleDecl.Open](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synmoduledecl.html#Open)

SynModuleDecl.Open 
Open 
 An 'open' definition within a module

### [SynModuleDecl.Attributes](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synmoduledecl.html#Attributes)

SynModuleDecl.Attributes 
Attributes 
 An attribute definition within a module, for assembly and .NET module attributes

### [SynSimplePat.Id](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synsimplepat.html#Id)

SynSimplePat.Id 
Id 
<pre> Indicates a simple pattern variable.

 altNameRefCell:
   Normally 'None' except for some compiler-generated variables in desugaring pattern matching.
   Pattern processing sets this reference for hidden variable introduced
   by desugaring pattern matching in arguments. The info indicates an
   alternative (compiler generated) identifier to be used because the
   name of the identifier is already bound.

 isCompilerGenerated: true if a compiler generated name
 isThisVal: true if 'this' variable in member
 isOptional: true if a '?' is in front of the name</pre>

### [SynSimplePat.Typed](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-synsimplepat.html#Typed)

SynSimplePat.Typed

 A type annotated simple pattern

### [SynTypeDefnSimpleRepr.General](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-syntypedefnsimplerepr.html#General)

SynTypeDefnSimpleRepr.General 
General 
 An object oriented type definition. This is not a parse-tree form, but represents the core
 type representation which the type checker splits out from the "ObjectModel" cases of type definitions.

SynTypeDefnSimpleRepr.LibraryOnlyILAssembly 
LibraryOnlyILAssembly 
 A type defined by using an IL assembly representation. Only used in FSharp.Core.

 F# syntax: "type X = (# "..."#)

### [SyntaxVisitorBase<'T>.VisitExpr](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntax-syntaxvisitorbase-1.html#VisitExpr)

SyntaxVisitorBase<'T>.VisitExpr 
VisitExpr 
<pre> Controls the behavior when a SynExpr is reached; it can just do
          defaultTraverse(expr)      if you have no special logic for this node, and want the default processing to pick which sub-node to dive deeper into
 or can inject non-default behavior, which might incorporate:
          traverseSynExpr(subExpr)   to recurse deeper on some particular sub-expression based on your own logic
 path helps to track AST nodes that were passed during traversal</pre>

### [IdentTrivia.IsOriginalNotationWithParen](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntaxtrivia-identtrivia.html#IsOriginalNotationWithParen)

IdentTrivia.IsOriginalNotationWithParen 
IsOriginalNotationWithParen

### [IdentTrivia.OriginalNotation](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntaxtrivia-identtrivia.html#OriginalNotation)

IdentTrivia.OriginalNotation 
OriginalNotation 
 The ident originally had a different notation.
 Example: a + b
 The operator ident will be compiled into "op_Addition", while the original notation was "+"

### [IdentTrivia.OriginalNotationWithParen](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntaxtrivia-identtrivia.html#OriginalNotationWithParen)

IdentTrivia.OriginalNotationWithParen 
OriginalNotationWithParen 
 The ident originally had a different notation and parenthesis
 Example: let (>=>) a b = ...
 The operator ident will be compiled into "op_GreaterEqualsGreater", while the original notation was ">=>" and had parenthesis

### [IdentTrivia.HasParenthesis](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-syntaxtrivia-identtrivia.html#HasParenthesis)

IdentTrivia.HasParenthesis 
HasParenthesis 
 The ident had parenthesis
 Example: let (|Odd|Even|) = ...
 The active pattern ident will be "|Odd|Even|", while originally there were parenthesis.

### [Line](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-line.html)

Line 
 Functions related to converting between lines indexed at 0 and 1 
Line.fromZ 
fromZ 
Line.toZ 
toZ

### [Line.fromZ](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-line.html#fromZ)

Line.fromZ 
fromZ 
 Convert a line number from zero-based line counting (used by Visual Studio) to one-based line counting (used internally in the F# compiler and in F# error messages)

### [Line.toZ](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-line.html#toZ)

Line.toZ 
toZ 
 Convert a line number from one-based line counting (used internally in the F# compiler and in F# error messages) to zero-based line counting (used by Visual Studio)

Range.IsSynthetic 
IsSynthetic 
 Synthetic marks ranges which are produced by intermediate compilation phases. This
 bit signifies that the range covers something that should not be visible to language
 service operations like dot-completion.

### [range.EndColumn](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#EndColumn)

range.EndColumn 
EndColumn 
 The column number for the end position of the range

### [range.Start](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#Start)

range.Start 
Start 
 The start position of the range

### [range.EndRange](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#EndRange)

range.EndRange 
EndRange 
 The empty range that is located at the end position of the range

### [range.StartRange](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#StartRange)

range.StartRange 
StartRange 
 The empty range that is located at the start position of the range

### [range.IsSynthetic](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#IsSynthetic)

range.IsSynthetic 
IsSynthetic 
 Synthetic marks ranges which are produced by intermediate compilation phases. This
 bit signifies that the range covers something that should not be visible to language
 service operations like dot-completion.

### [range.StartLine](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#StartLine)

range.StartLine 
StartLine 
 The start line of the range

### [range.End](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#End)

range.End 
End 
 The end position of the range

### [range.EndLine](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#EndLine)

range.EndLine 
EndLine 
 The line number for the end position of the range

### [range.FileName](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#FileName)

range.FileName 
FileName 
 The file name for the file of the range

### [range.StartColumn](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#StartColumn)

range.StartColumn 
StartColumn 
 The start column of the range

### [range.Zero](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-text-range-0.html#Zero)

range.Zero 
Zero 
 The range where all values are zero

### [FSharpLineTokenizer](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-tokenization-fsharplinetokenizer.html)

FSharpLineTokenizer 
 Object to tokenize a line of F# source code, starting with the given lexState.  The lexState should be FSharpTokenizerLexState.Initial for
 the first line of text. Returns an array of ranges of the text and two enumerations categorizing the
 tokens and characters covered by that range, i.e. FSharpTokenColorKind and FSharpTokenCharKind.  The enumerations
 are somewhat adhoc but useful enough to give good colorization options to the user in an IDE.

 A new lexState is also returned.  An IDE-plugin should in general cache the lexState
 values for each line of the edited code. 
FSharpLineTokenizer.ScanToken 
ScanToken 
FSharpLineTokenizer.ColorStateOfLexState 
ColorStateOfLexState 
FSharpLineTokenizer.LexStateOfColorState 
LexStateOfColorState

### [FSharpLineTokenizer.ScanToken](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-tokenization-fsharplinetokenizer.html#ScanToken)

FSharpLineTokenizer.ScanToken 
ScanToken 
 Scan one token from the line

### [FSharpLineTokenizer.ColorStateOfLexState](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-tokenization-fsharplinetokenizer.html#ColorStateOfLexState)

FSharpLineTokenizer.ColorStateOfLexState 
ColorStateOfLexState 
 Get the color state from the lexer state

### [FSharpLineTokenizer.LexStateOfColorState](https://fsharp.github.io/fsharp-compiler-docs/reference/fsharp-compiler-tokenization-fsharplinetokenizer.html#LexStateOfColorState)

FSharpLineTokenizer.LexStateOfColorState 
LexStateOfColorState 
 Get a default lexer state for a color state.

 NOTE: This may result in an inaccurate lexer state
 not taking into account things such as the #if/#endif and string interpolation context
 within the file
